sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Achilles tendinopathy is a load-related tendon condition causing pain, stiffness, and reduced push-off function. The key is distinguishing tendinopathy from rupture, inflammatory disease, or major retrocalcaneal pathology.

sections.title: Clinical Presentation
sections.content:
- Pain and stiffness in the Achilles region, often worse with first steps
- Pain during running, stairs, hills, or repeated calf loading
- Tendon thickening or local tenderness
- Reduced calf endurance or hopping tolerance

sections.title: Considerations
sections.content:
- Escalate for suspected rupture, sudden power loss, marked swelling, or inflammatory/systemic features
- Insertional and midportion Achilles pain behave differently and should not be managed identically
- Complete rest usually delays tendon capacity recovery

sections.title: Management - Primary
sections.content:
- Screen rupture risk, ability to plantarflex, and whether weight-bearing is safe
- Reduce provocative loading and provide simple pain relief and activity modification within scope
- Escalate if rupture, severe swelling, or major function loss is suspected

sections.title: Management - Advanced
sections.content:
- Distinguish insertional from midportion presentation and irritability
- Use staged calf loading, tendon education, and footwear or heel-lift discussion where appropriate
- Refer for imaging or specialist review when rupture concern, major insertional irritability, or poor progress persists

sections.title: Additional Notes
sections.content: - Tendon recovery is usually driven by consistent progressive loading, not passive treatment alone
